name: MIDI MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server enabling AI agents to generate and control MIDI (Musical Instrument Digital Interface) — creating melodies, chord progressions, rhythmic patterns, and full compositions in MIDI format that can be played through software synthesizers, DAWs, or hardware MIDI devices. Bridges AI-generated music concepts into the universal MIDI protocol.
use_cases: Generating MIDI melodies and chord progressions from music composition agents
Creating rhythmic patterns and drum beats from production agents
Composing background music for multimedia projects from creative agents
Exploring musical ideas and harmonics from musical AI research agents
not_for: Audio recording and processing (MIDI is note data, not audio)
Finished audio production without DAW rendering
best_when: An AI agent workflow needs to generate musical notation in MIDI format — particularly for composing music that will be rendered through DAWs, virtual instruments, or hardware synthesizers.
avoid_when: You need finished audio output — MIDI requires a synthesizer/DAW to render sound. For direct audio generation, use music AI services instead.

auth.notes: No authentication — local MIDI file generation tool.

pricing.notes: Free open source MIDI generation MCP.

known_agent_gotchas: MIDI knowledge required — agents need to understand notes, tempo, time signatures, velocity
Generated MIDI requires playback software (DAW, VirtualMIDISynth) to hear output
Musical theory constraints (harmony, chord progressions) must be encoded in agent prompts
MIDI file format version compatibility should be verified for target software
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local MIDI file generation. No credentials. No external data transmission. MIDI files are data, not executable. Minimal security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local generation only. No credentials. No network access needed. MIDI data is safe.
